title: Payena junghuhniana
intro: Payena junghuhniana, commonly known as the Payena tree, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Sapotaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Payena junghuhniana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Payena junghuhniana include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including colds, flu, and respiratory infections.
uses: Payena junghuhniana is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae. However, information on its specific medicinal uses is limited. Payena junghuhniana is native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Indonesia and Malaysia, but detailed studies on its medicinal properties are scarce.
